methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mbodi AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xavier Chi that has raised $3M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader sector universe of nine private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and one public comparable, though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to support a reliable peer-median approach.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework here. Using the $3M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $8M. This method reflects observed stepping patterns across early-stage capital rounds but does not account for company-specific operational or market differentiation.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints: while 11 robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader dataset, none satisfy narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables. This limits triangulation to a single method. The analysis supports a valuation range of $6M (low) to $9M (high) with a median point of $8M. Primary uncertainties include the absence of near-peer revenue or growth metrics, the reliance on a single valuation method, and the stage-agnostic nature of the step-up multiplier applied.
